mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates for future cash flows, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during non-recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in consumer spending and residential construction indicates synchronized domestic demand growth, which drives corporate earnings revisions upward and produces positive quarterly equity returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their 12-month moving averages
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over individual series (F-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates into higher short-term corporate profitability and positive one-week equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases are followed by negative 5-day returns in >65% of observations during early-cycle phases, or the signal's predictive accuracy vanishes when inflation exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces expectations of monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >55% of triggered instances, or the inflation threshold shows no statistical significance in predicting return direction (logit coefficient p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent payroll growth and unemployment rate declines confirm robust labor market tightening, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, driving positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal predicts negative returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth reflects expanding economic activity and corporate revenue bases, which systematically translates into positive quarterly equity returns through earnings growth channels.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth is followed by negative 60-day returns in >60% of observations, or the series exhibits a Rank IC below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply-side weakness and falling business activity, triggering risk aversion and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in >55% of triggered instances, or the decline threshold fails to predict return direction with statistical significance (p > 0.05).
